title: Quadlet .network systemd unit succeeds when it should fail
body:
### Issue Description

When creating a podman network with a quadlet network file, if given a bad config the systemd unit will suggest success when in fact podman failed to create the network.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a .network quadlet file with incorrect config (Error: dhcp ipam driver is set but subnets are given)
``` bash
[Network]
NetworkName=traefik
Subnet=10.90.0.0/24
Gateway=10.90.0.1
Driver=bridge
IPAMDriver=dhcp
IPRange=10.90.0.0/24
Label=TraefikNetwork=true
```
2. Copy file to /etc/containers/systemd, systemctl daemon-reload, systemctl start traefik-network.service
3. View systemctl status traefik-network.service


### Describe the results you received

Service is active and Main PID: 1992 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)


### Describe the results you expected

service failed with error
Error: dhcp ipam driver is set but subnets are given

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
@bnwhorton Thanks for reporting this.

I tried to reproduce this issue on Fedora 38 with 4.8.3 and with Fedora 39 with 4.9.3. I used both a Quadlet file and a direct call to Podman. I also tried both as a user and as root.

From what I can see, in some cases, the command finished successfully created the network and returned 0. The only case it failed (both the command the the systemd unit) is when I ran it as root on Fedora 38 and Podman 4.8.3.

So, this is either a change in Podman's behavior, or a change in the environment. In either case, not a Quadlet or systemd issue.
Strange behavior but thanks for looking in to it, ill close this and open a new one if i can provide more info or something actionable.
